title: Fitzpatrick's Valspar Redemption: First PGA Tour Win Since 2023
excerpt: Matt Fitzpatrick ended a near three-year PGA Tour drought with a clutch birdie on the 72nd hole at Innisbrook, setting up an electric Masters return.
body:
From Sawgrass Heartbreak to Copperhead Glory

Seven days after watching Cameron Young steal The Players Championship from under his nose at the 17th island green, Matt Fitzpatrick returned to Florida and made amends in emphatic fashion. The 31-year-old Sheffield native closed bogey-free with a 3-under 68 at Innisbrook Resort's fearsome Copperhead Course, posting 11-under 273 to edge David Lipsky by a single stroke and claim the 2026 Valspar Championship.

It was Fitzpatrick's first PGA Tour victory since the 2023 RBC Heritage — a drought stretching nearly three years that had cast real doubt over whether the 2022 US Open champion could rediscover his top-level form. He answered emphatically.

$1,638,000
Winner's Cheque
-11
Winning Score
500
FedEx Cup Points
3rd
FedEx Cup Rank

Drama on the Copperhead Back Nine

Entering Sunday three shots off the pace, Fitzpatrick needed the leaders to crack. They did. Overnight contenders Sungjae Im and Brandt Snedeker combined for a catastrophic eight-over final round, ceding the stage entirely. Fitzpatrick and Lipsky traded birdies across the closing stretch, setting up a grandstand finish.

The decisive blows came at the par-3 15th, where Fitzpatrick holed a stunning 30-foot birdie to draw level, then produced a masterclass on the 72nd hole. A 303-yard drive split the narrow fairway, followed by a pitching wedge to 14 feet, and Fitzpatrick poured in the tournament-winning putt. Lipsky, from the rough at the last, could not match him. Fitzpatrick had posted just four bogeys all week — the fewest in the field.

Augusta Beckons

With the Masters at Augusta National just two weeks away, the timing could scarcely be better. Fitzpatrick has recorded two top-10s in ten Augusta starts and has never missed the cut. He arrives in Georgia ranked sixth in the world, brimming with confidence after back-to-back weekends of contention at TPC Sawgrass and Innisbrook.

A difficult 2024 — winless, off-form, absent from leaderboards — now feels like a distant memory. A quiet revival that began midway through 2025 has gathered irresistible momentum ahead of the year's first major.

The Putter That Made the Difference

Fitzpatrick's weapon of choice was a Bettinardi DASS prototype — a mirror of the Yes! Tracy II putter he has used since the age of 16. Those two crucial long-range birdie putts at the 15th and 18th were the difference. His third PGA Tour title and ninth worldwide victory confirms the Sheffield man is back — and highly dangerous heading to Augusta National.
